text.title: AI Diff Size Discipline Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped ai-diff-size-discipline work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: AI bundles a rename refactor with a new feature; reviewer cannot tell which change broke the test.
antipattern.root-cause: Author prompted "add feature X and clean up while you're there"; AI complied.
antipattern.fix: Split into 2 PRs: refactor first (mechanical, easy review), feature second (small diff, focused review). See rule single-unit.

antipattern.symptom: 1500-line rename diff that AI claims is "mostly mechanical" hides 3 logic changes.
antipattern.root-cause: No separation between rename commit and logic-change commits.
antipattern.fix: Require rename as a single commit with --no-author-fix marker; logic changes ride a separate commit. See rule loc-cap.

antipattern.symptom: AI emits 200 LOC of feature code with 0 LOC of tests; reviewer cannot tell what the feature is supposed to do.
antipattern.fix: Reject diffs where loc_test_touched == 0 for feature/fix units; require at least 1 test per semantic unit. See rule single-unit.

antipattern.symptom: PR description says "fix login bug"; diff also touches billing + onboarding; author claims "saw they were broken too".
antipattern.root-cause: No scope gate at PR open.
antipattern.fix: Validator compares PR description scope vs file paths touched; mismatch > 20% triggers verdict=split. See rule single-unit.
